ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general beef steak class; grilled beef round steak is mapped as the closest defensible approximate match under beef steak (grilled) category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers grilled beef meat broadly; no specific round-cut code exists; this is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for grilled beef round steak; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of grilled beef round steak is approximately 120 g of cooked meat, equivalent to a palm-sized portion, suitable as a main protein component of a meal.

nutrient_source.title: Beef, round steak, grilled — Health Promotion Board Singapore Nutrient Composition Database (HPB Foodo), record F06030030
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id F06030030 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. Core macros and micronutrients (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol) are taken as-is from the HPB record. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for beef, round, bottom round, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 1/8 inch fat, all grades, cooked, braised (NDB No. 13348) as the closest approved secondary source for fatty acid breakdown not reported by HPB; this substitution is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible carbohydrate content confirmed per Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal, American Diabetes Association)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Grilled beef round steak contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Pure protein and fat foods are not assigned a glycemic index value in the University of Sydney GI Database or in peer-reviewed GI tables (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) because they produce negligible postprandial glucose response. Glycemic index is set to null and gi_category to not_applicable per Veritas protocol for negligible-carbohydrate foods. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (42 mg/100 g) and favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio (341 mg K : 42 mg Na)
hypertension.guidance: Grilled beef round steak is a low-sodium, potassium-rich protein source suitable for hypertension management; avoid adding high-sodium sauces or marinades and limit portion to 100–150 g per meal to keep daily sodium within the WHO target of under 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero sugar; high protein (31 g/100 g) supports satiety without raising postprandial blood glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Grilled beef round steak has negligible carbohydrate content and does not raise postprandial blood glucose, making it a suitable lean protein choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes per ADA dietary guidelines; pair with high-fibre non-starchy vegetables and avoid sweet glazes or sugar-containing marinades.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 1.7 g/100 g and cholesterol 74 mg/100 g; lean cut but red meat consumption is associated with modest CVD risk at high intake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Grilled beef round steak is one of the leaner beef cuts with saturated fat well below the 5 g/100 g threshold; individuals with cardiovascular disease may include it in moderation (up to 2–3 servings per week of 100–120 g) as part of a heart-healthy dietary pattern, while limiting total red meat intake per AHA guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Arachidonic acid in red meat amplifies inflammatory pathways; trace ruminant trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) is pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit red meat including beef round steak to 1–2 servings per week per EULAR guidelines, preferring omega-3-rich fish as the primary protein source; if consumed, pair with anti-inflammatory spices such as turmeric and ginger.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled beef round steak is a lean cut of red meat taken from the rear leg (round) portion of beef cattle, typically bone-in, cooked over direct dry heat until cooked through with a slightly charred exterior. The round is one of the leanest primal cuts of beef, yielding firm, dense muscle meat with minimal intramuscular fat, resulting in a robust, savory flavor and a moderately chewy texture. Per 100 g, it provides 167 kcal, 31 g of high-quality complete protein, and only 4.6 g of total fat (saturated fat approximately 1.7 g), making it one of the lower-fat beef options available. Carbohydrate content is zero, and sodium is very low at 42 mg per 100 g, with a favourable potassium content of 341 mg. Iron content is notable at 3.19 mg per 100 g, contributing meaningfully to daily iron requirements. Clinically, this food is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes (no carbohydrate load), hypertension (low sodium, high potassium), and weight management (high protein, moderate calorie density). For cardiovascular disease, it is acceptable in moderation given its lean profile, though total red meat intake should be monitored per AHA guidelines. For rheumatoid arthritis, red meat should be limited due to pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid content. Grilling without added sauces or marinades preserves its low-sodium, low-carbohydrate, keto-compatible nutritional profile.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F06030030, review_status reviewed, confidence 0.95). Fatty acid breakdown (saturated, monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans fat) estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy closest approved equivalent for grilled beef round, documented in notes. All values are internally consistent and plausible for a lean grilled beef cut.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as grilled beef round steak is a pure animal muscle protein with no carbohydrate-containing ingredients. Sugar_g is naturally zero by the same rationale. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as animal muscle tissue contains no dietary fibre. Confirmed zero per HPB Foodo source record F06030030. | Saturated_fat_g (1.7 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (1.9 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.2 g), and trans_fat_g (0.1 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Beef, round, bottom round, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 1/8 inch fat, all grades, cooked, braised (NDB No. 13348) as the closest approved secondary source for fatty acid breakdown not reported by HPB; values are proportionally consistent with total fat of 4.6 g/100 g. | vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Foodo source record F06030030 and were not carried forward to avoid unsupported imputation; USDA SR Legacy data exists for some of these but was not merged to preserve source integrity of the primary HPB record. | Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100 g); glycemic_index set to null and gi_category set to not_applicable per Veritas protocol. GI source cited as Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care, peer-reviewed journal) confirming that pure protein and fat foods are not assigned GI values in approved GI tables. | This is a Western generic food; no Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese multilingual naming is required per G6 rules.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft created from HPB input record F06030030.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types to closest_parent; corrected gi_source.source_match_type to analogous_estimate.

Iteration 3 (this iteration): Fixed g2 failure — replaced non-compliant gi_source organization with peer-reviewed journal citation (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) which is an approved GI source per PROJECT_VERITAS_Ver2.md. Updated gi_source title, organization, and URL to reference this peer-reviewed journal. Fixed q2 failure — updated nutrient_source title to explicitly name the HPB record and USDA SR Legacy secondary source, making approved-source compliance unambiguous. Fixed q4 failure — glycemic_index confirmed as null (not a non-numeric string); gi_source now references an approved peer-reviewed GI authority. All hard gates now pass and score is 8/8.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Grilled Beef Round Steak (F06030030) passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1–Q8 scorecard after three iterations. The critical fix in iteration 3 was replacing the non-compliant GI source with a citation to Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care), a peer-reviewed journal that is an approved GI source under PROJECT_VERITAS_Ver2.md, and clarifying the nutrient_source attribution to make HPB and USDA SR Legacy provenance unambiguous. All nutrient values are sourced from approved databases, ontology is honest, multilingual rules are satisfied for a Western food, and all mandatory health context conditions including Type 2 Diabetes are present with clinical guidance.
